AMD.reasoning: TREND ASSESSMENT: AMD daily trend is unambiguously strong. EMA alignment is perfectly fanned (EMA5 325.6 > EMA10 308.0 > EMA21 279.0 > EMA50 247.5), ADX at 73.57 with +DI 56.62 vs -DI 10.51 confirms an extremely powerful uptrend. Recent price action shows higher lows intact (low 200.62 to current 337-345 range). Daily volume bias is up and OBV is not diverging from price. MOMENTUM ASSESSMENT: Daily ROC is strongly positive and accelerating across all windows (5d +11.09%, 10d +30.6%, 21d +71.96%) — the clearest momentum expansion signal possible. Daily MACD histogram +6.87 (MACD 31.34 > signal 24.47) confirms daily momentum is expanding. ENTRY ZONE CONCERN: This is a trend-continuation strategy requiring pullbacks to the 10 or 21 EMA. Current price (~345) is 9.4% above EMA10 (308) and 20.8% above EMA21 (279) — significantly overextended for a clean pullback entry. HOURLY DIVERGENCE WARNING: Hourly MACD histogram is -1.69 (signal line above MACD line), and critically, hourly -DI (33.53) exceeds +DI (20.45) — short-term intraday trend is actually bearish, diverging from the daily trend. This is an early warning of near-term exhaustion consistent with wiki context noting prior RSI 91 overbought readings and bearish hourly STC cross. RSI at 82 is deeply overbought. VERDICT: The macro regime (mixed-to-cautious with AI tailwind intact) and AMD's fundamental momentum support the bullish thesis, but the entry is premature at current levels. A pullback to the 10 EMA zone (~308-315) would provide the textbook trend-continuation entry with hourly re-alignment. Confidence is moderate — the trend is real but the setup is not yet at an actionable entry. Waiting for hourly momentum to re-align with daily before entry.
